description: This chart presents the mechanical properties of five low-density nano-bainitic Al-C steels (S1-S5) under various austenitization (T_A) and austempering (T_B) conditions, displaying ultimate tensile strength (UTS, black squares), yield strength (Rp0.2, blue inverted triangles), elongation (El, red triangles), and Vickers hardness (HV30, black circles with labeled values). The x-axis identifies specific steel grades paired with heat treatment parameters: S1 and S2 at 1150°C/350°C and 1150°C/300°C; S3 at 1220°C/350°C and 1220°C/270°C; S4 at 1150°C/350°C, 1150°C/320°C, and 1250°C/350°C; and S5 at 1150°C/365°C and 1150°C/335°C. Key trends reveal that S4 achieves the highest elongation (10-12%) at T_A 1150°C/T_B 350°C while maintaining high strength (~1360 MPa), whereas S3 exhibits severely reduced ductility (2.4%) at 270°C. Lower austempering temperatures generally increase strength but decrease elongation across all alloys. Hardness values range from 443 to 500 HV30. The data demonstrate that Ti-microalloyed S4 exhibits the optimal strength-ductility balance, supporting the conclusion that careful control of alloy chemistry and heat treatment parameters is critical for maximizing the product of strength and elongation in nano-bainitic steels.
